text:
There once was a man who could talk to animals. He lived in a small village where everyone loved his tales of wildlife conversations.

One autumn morning, he sat under an oak talking to a squirrel. "How's it going?" he asked, smiling.

"Same old, nuts," the squirrel replied, dropping a nut.

"That sounds like a plan," chuckled the man. The squirrel giggled.

A wise old owl swooped down nearby. "What are you up to?" it asked.

"A tale," said the man. "A story."

"Ah, what sort of a story?" inquired the owl.

"Well," the man began, "I once met a wise owl. After listening to my life's adventures, it said, 'Never bother capturing a fish; you'd never tire of nibbling.'"

The owl laughed heartily. "Such wisdom! And such humor."

Later that day, someone left out fish on the man's porch. He had a good laugh!

One evening
